veg_meal.source: FAO Food Emissions Database; Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
veg_meal.notes: 1 Indian thali: dal + sabzi + 2 roti + rice, ~600g cooked weight total. Meal composition varies widely; factor represents a typical middle-income urban thali.

non_veg_meal_chicken.source: FAO Food Emissions Database (poultry: ~6.9 kg CO2e/kg live weight); Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
non_veg_meal_chicken.notes: 1 serving chicken curry with rice: ~100g boneless chicken + ~150g cooked rice + sabzi, ~450g total cooked weight. Poultry factor includes feed, farm, and processing; rice and vegetable accompaniments included.

non_veg_meal_mutton.source: FAO Food Emissions Database (sheep/goat: ~39 kg CO2e/kg, India-adjusted downward for goat vs sheep); Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
non_veg_meal_mutton.notes: 1 serving mutton/goat curry with rice: ~100g boneless mutton + ~150g cooked rice, ~400g total cooked weight. Indian mutton is predominantly goat; factor adjusted below the global sheep average.

non_veg_meal_fish.source: FAO Food Emissions Database; LEAP (Livestock Environmental Assessment and Performance) Partnership 2020; Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
non_veg_meal_fish.notes: 1 serving fish curry with rice: ~100g fish + ~150g cooked rice, ~400g total cooked weight. Indian fish consumption is a mix of coastal catch and freshwater aquaculture; factor averaged across common species.

egg_single.source: FAO Food Emissions Database (eggs: ~4.2 kg CO2e/kg); Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
egg_single.notes: 1 large whole egg, ~60g. Factor includes feed, farm operations, and processing but excludes cooking energy.

dairy_serving_250ml.source: FAO Food Emissions Database (dairy milk: ~3.2 kg CO2e/kg, India-adjusted for local feed mix); Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
dairy_serving_250ml.notes: 250ml whole cow's milk. Adjusted for Indian mixed-breed cattle and feed composition which typically results in a lower emission intensity than global OECD averages.

paneer_serving_100g.source: FAO Food Emissions Database; milk-to-paneer conversion ratio applied to dairy milk factor; Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
paneer_serving_100g.notes: 100g paneer; approximately 0.5 kg whole milk required (5:1 milk-to-paneer conversion ratio by weight). Factor includes dairy farming emissions allocated to paneer fraction.

dal_serving.source: FAO Food Emissions Database (pulses: ~0.9 kg CO2e/kg dry weight); Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
dal_serving.notes: 200ml cooked dal (~70g dry lentils/split pulses + water + tempering oil and spices). Pulse factor includes nitrogen fixation credit; tempering oil and spices add a small additional contribution.

rice_serving.source: FAO Food Emissions Database (rice: ~4.0 kg CO2e/kg paddy including CH4); IRRI rice GHG emissions study 2021; Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
rice_serving.notes: 1 cup cooked rice, ~75g uncooked dry weight (~190g cooked). Includes methane (CH4) from anaerobic decomposition in flooded paddy fields, which is the dominant emission source for rice cultivation.

snack_processed.source: FAO Food Emissions Database; lifecycle analysis for packaged foods (India-adjusted); Indian dietary survey data, methodology in README
snack_processed.notes: 1 packet processed snack ~50g (e.g. chips, biscuits, namkeen). Factor covers raw ingredient farming, industrial processing, and primary packaging; retail transport and refrigeration excluded.
